title: Finviz Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Finviz pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Finviz Pricing Overview

Finviz offers one of the most straightforward pricing models in the stock screener market: free or paid, with no middle ground. The platform costs nothing to start with the Free tier, and $39.50 per month to upgrade to Elite. Founded in 2007 and headquartered in New York, Finviz has built its reputation on delivering a powerful, no-nonsense screener that appeals to swing traders, fundamental analysts, and traders who want an instant visual overview of the markets without breaking the bank.

The pricing reflects Finviz's core philosophy: the free tier is genuinely usable (not a crippled trial), and the paid tier removes barriers for serious traders who need real-time data and advanced filtering. This simplicity is refreshing compared to competitors who layer features across four or five pricing tiers.

All Finviz Plans Compared

Finviz keeps it simple with two distinct offerings:

Feature Free Elite
Price $0/month $39.50/month
Stock Screener Access ✓ ✓
60+ Filters ✓ ✓
Heat Maps ✓ ✓
News Feed & Insider Data ✓ ✓
Data Refresh Rate 15-20 min delay Real-time
Backtesting ✗ ✓
Alerts ✗ ✓
API Access ✗ ✓
Banner Ads Yes No

The comparison reveals Finviz's clear value proposition: the Free plan gives you the core screener experience with minimal limitations for basic research, while Elite unlocks real-time data, automation tools (alerts and backtesting), and removes ads—critical upgrades for active traders.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Finviz does offer a true free tier (not a trial), and it's genuinely one of the best free stock screeners available. Millions of traders use it daily without upgrading, and that's no accident.

What you get with the Free plan:

  • Full access to 60+ stock screening filters across fundamentals, technicals, and volatility
  • Iconic heat maps showing market structure by sector and market cap—invaluable for visual traders
  • Real-time insider trading data and news aggregation for every stock
  • Fast, responsive screener performance (Finviz's screening speed is best-in-class)
  • Fundamental and technical analysis charts (basic functionality)

What you give up:

  • Data is delayed by 15-20 minutes, not real-time
  • Banner ads throughout the platform (not intrusive, but present)
  • No backtesting, alerts, or API access
  • No charting comparable to TradingView or thinkorswim

For fundamental analysis, swing traders who don't mind a 15-minute data delay, and anyone who just wants a quick market overview, the Free plan is absolutely worth starting with. The 15-minute delay is only problematic if you're day trading or need to execute on real-time signals.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Finviz's pricing is refreshingly transparent—there are no hidden fees, data subscriptions, or surprise charges. What you see is what you pay.

However, consider these indirect costs:

  • No annual discount (yet): Finviz bills monthly at $39.50/month. If annual billing were available, you might save 10-15% (common in SaaS), but Finviz doesn't currently offer this. That's $474/year paid monthly with no bulk discount.
  • Limited charting: If you rely on advanced charting, you'll likely need a second platform (TradingView, thinkorswim, etc.), adding to total tool costs.
  • No mobile app: Finviz is browser-only with poor mobile optimization. If you need on-the-go access, you may need a mobile screener app as a secondary tool.
  • Exchange fees: Not Finviz's fees, but be aware that some data (particularly real-time stock data in Elite) is sourced from exchanges, which Finviz includes in the Elite subscription.

The bottom line: the pricing is flat and honest. No add-ons, no upsells within the tier itself.

Finviz Pricing vs Competitors

How does Finviz stack up against other screeners? Here's a competitive snapshot:

Platform Free Price Paid Price Best For
Finviz $0 $39.50/mo Visual market overviews, fundamental screening
TradingView $0 $15-$65/mo* Advanced charting, multi-asset screeners
TD Ameritrade thinkorswim Free (with account) Included Options traders, integrated broker platform
Yahoo Finance $0 $0 Basic screening, portfolio tracking
Barchart $0 $99-$199/mo* Futures and options screening

*Competitor pricing is approximate and subject to change. Consult their sites for current rates.

Finviz's competitive advantage: It's the cheapest paid screener for stock-focused traders ($39.50/mo), and its free tier is more feature-rich than most competitors' free plans. TradingView is more affordable at $15/month but lacks Finviz's iconic heat maps and insider data. thinkorswim is free for account holders but requires maintaining a brokerage account. Barchart excels at futures and options but costs 2-5x more. For fundamental and technical stock screening, Finviz offers the best value.

Is Finviz Worth the Price?

Free Plan: Absolutely worth it—if you're a hobbyist, swing trader, or fundamental analyst who can tolerate a 15-minute data delay. The screener speed, heat maps, and included insider data make it a no-brainer starter platform. Millions of traders use the free tier as their primary research tool.

Elite Plan ($39.50/mo): Worth it if you're a serious trader who needs real-time data, backtesting, alerts, or API access. For $39.50/month ($474/year), you get automated screening signals and the ability to build trading systems—that's remarkably affordable compared to competitors. It becomes a no-brainer upgrade if you:

  • Trade actively and need real-time quotes and signals
  • Want to automate screening with API access for algo trading
  • Use backtesting to validate strategies before trading live
  • Value the ad-free experience during long research sessions

When to skip Finviz altogether: If you're an options trader (Finviz has no options chain data), trade international markets (US stocks only), need professional-grade charting, or prefer an integrated broker platform with built-in screeners (thinkorswim). Otherwise, Finviz should be in your toolkit.

How to Save on Finviz

Finviz doesn't currently offer annual billing discounts, promotional codes, or student plans. However, here's how to minimize costs:

  • Start with Free: Use the free tier for 2-4 weeks to test the screener before committing to Elite. This is the lowest-risk approach.
  • Combine with free alternatives: Use Finviz's free tier alongside TradingView's free charting to avoid paying for two screeners.
  • Use the API strategically: If you're an Elite member building trading systems, the API access (included) can help you automate screening, reducing the time spent in other tools.
  • Watch for rare promotions: While not common, Finviz occasionally offers discounted annual subscriptions during market downturns or on special occasion emails. Check their homepage and sign up for email updates.
  • Justify the $39.50/mo cost: If you're day trading and this tool helps you find one profitable trade per month, the Elite subscription pays for itself.

In short: Finviz doesn't game its pricing with promotional cliffs or tiers, which is refreshing. Your best saving strategy is maximizing the free tier value, then upgrading to Elite when you're ready to trade seriously.

Ready to try Finviz? Start with the free plan at finviz.com, and upgrade to Elite when real-time data and backtesting become critical for your strategy. For a full feature review, read our complete Finviz review. Comparing screeners? See our stock screener comparison chart for side-by-side analysis of Finviz, TradingView, and competitors.
